description: Repository layer query: pinned DB decision surfaces; scoped open question surfaces; relevant beliefs depend on query token overlap
query: How should I structure my repository layer?

description: Alias 'k8s' resolves to Kubernetes entity
query: what's my k8s deploy story?

description: Alias 'kube' drives text search retrieval
query: how does kube schedule pods across our CI pipeline nodes?

description: Alias 'GHA' (short form) drives text-search retrieval of b-ci-gha-entity
query: GHA secrets config

description: Verbose 400+ char query.
query: ok so I've been thinking about this for a while and I know we've talked about infrastructure before but I'm circling back because the team had some pushback in the last review and honestly I'm not sure what the right call is anymore. Basically the question is how we should handle rolling deploys for the new auth service when it lands — should we do Kubernetes native rolling updates or something more custom, and does it matter that the service holds session state in memory?

description: Query in a domain the user has no beliefs about: personaPrelude only, no scope/relevant leakage
query: what's a good way to grade a 1909-S VDB penny without sending it to PCGS?

description: Writing query surfaces writing beliefs only; no code leakage
query: How should I open the next chapter with Mara?

description: 'Redis' in writing scope returns character, not datastore
query: What's Redis doing in chapter three?

description: 'Redis' in code scope returns datastore, not character
query: What are we using Redis for?

description: With scope=['code','writing'], both Redis beliefs should surface — one per scope
query: What's Redis doing?

description: Superseded SQLAlchemy belief never surfaces
query: how's our SQLAlchemy setup these days?
notes: b-db-decision is pinned so verified in pinnedFacts. shouldOnlyInclude: [] subsumes the old mustExclude for b-sqlalchemy-superseded — it can't appear because only [] is allowed.

description: Empty query: persona + scope prelude + pinned facts injected, no relevant beliefs
Short query 'fastify?' passes raw to text search. Belief is pinned so verified in pinnedFacts.
query: fastify?

description: Other-user beliefs never leak
query: what language do I write in?

description: listPinnedOpenQuestions returns only pinned+active questions for the given scope
query: any open questions?

description: Alias 'TS' drives text-search retrieval.
query: TS strict mode question about unknown types

description: Alias 'Mongo' drives text-search retrieval.
query: what's the Mongo pattern we're using for collections?

description: Alias 'ReactJS' resolves to b-react-entity via text search — genuine retrieval proof
query: how should I structure my ReactJS components here?

description: Alias 'protagonist' retrieves b-protagonist-entity (pinned). b-pov-shift-entity (non-pinned) proves text search fired.
query: what does the protagonist want, and does their viewpoint stay consistent in act two?

description: Direct canonical_name 'Mara' retrieves protagonist. b-protagonist-entity is pinned.
query: tell me about Mara's backstory with her father

description: Alias 'POV' drives text-search retrieval of b-pov-shift-entity (non-pinned, writing scope)
query: POV rules for chapter 7

description: Natural-language proxy 'base class' retrieves b-composition-inheritance (non-pinned) via alias
query: should I use a base class hierarchy for these domain objects?

description: Natural-language proxy 'exceptions' retrieves b-error-handling (non-pinned) via alias
query: when should I throw exceptions versus return them?

description: b-hosting-resolved has resolved_at set — query matching its aliases must not surface it
query: should we self-host the auth service or go with Fly.io?

description: Code-scope query must not surface writing-scope open questions
query: any unresolved decisions on this sprint?

description: Persona prelude contains substantive content tokens derived from universal preferences
query: what's our stack?

description: Short query 'k8s'
query: k8s?

description: Whitespace-only query is treated as empty by queryExpander — no relevant beliefs
Weakly-related in-scope query does not surface tangential beliefs below minScore
query: thinking about observability and monitoring dashboards

description: user_edited belief scoped to domain:writing surfaces in domain:code via listPinnedFacts $or branch
query: summarize what you know about my preferences

description: Non-user-edited writing belief does NOT cross into code scope pinnedFacts
query: what are my writing preferences?

description: canonical_name match (weight 10) outranks content-only match (weight 1) in relevantBeliefs ordering
query: composition over inheritance — what's our pattern?

description: Typo 'typscript' does NOT match 'TypeScript' — documents text search limitation
query: how do I configure typscript strict mode?

description: ALL-CAPS 'REACTJS' still resolves via case-insensitive text index
query: REACTJS component patterns

description: Filler-heavy query — NLP extraction must cut through noise to find 'error handling'
query: ugh so like I was wondering you know about how we should think about error handling in our codebase because honestly it's been bugging me for a while and I'm not really sure what the right approach is here

description: Negation 'I don't want class hierarchies' still surfaces the composition belief — text search has no semantic negation
query: I don't want class hierarchies in this codebase, what should I use instead?

description: Single query spanning two topics surfaces both non-pinned beliefs
query: how should ReactJS components handle exceptions from async calls?

description: Compound query mentions 6 entities — tests whether 12-token cap in buildSearchQuery drops non-pinned targets
query: When our React frontend catches error handling exceptions, how should the Kubernetes pods running MongoDB TypeScript services report to GitHub Actions?

description: Dense 8+ entity query that deliberately exceeds the 12-token cap — tests which non-pinned beliefs survive extraction truncation
query: Set up our React components to use composition pattern with explicit error handling, wire GitHub Actions CI to deploy Kubernetes pods serving the MongoDB TypeScript API with functional pipeline transforms

description: Critical alias 'exceptions' appears after 6+ proper nouns — tests whether extraction order causes late-position tokens to fall past the 12-token cap
query: Given our React frontend, the GitHub Actions CI pipeline, the Kubernetes deployment, and the MongoDB TypeScript backend, how should we approach exceptions in the data layer?

description: 1-edit deletion 'eror' fuzzy-matches alias 'error' — prefix 'er' passes
query: eror handling patterns

description: Typo 'Reedis' fuzzy-matches alias 'Redis' in both scopes — scope filter returns only code belief
query: Reedis cache eviction strategy
notes: Without scope filtering, both b-redis-code and b-redis-writing would appear. This case verifies the $match stage is doing the discrimination, not the search stage.

description: 1-edit distance but prefix guard blocks — 'mango' shares edit distance 1 with 'mongo' but 'ma' ≠ 'mo'
query: mango connection pooling
notes: prefixLength: 2 is doing the blocking, not scope filtering.

description: Alias 'k8s' — typo 'k9s' fails because prefix 'k9' ≠ 'k8' — single-char substitution in short aliases hits prefix guard
query: k9s pod scheduling
notes: Text search missed it — k9s prefix 'k9' does not match 'k8'. This is the fundamental brittleness of single-character aliases.

description: Transposition 'typsecript' IS matched by Atlas (Damerau-Levenshtein, 1 edit)
query: typsecript compilation config

description: Query is a single misspelled term — 'mongod' is the only token, 1 edit from alias 'Mongo', prefix 'mo' passes
query: mongod

description: user:universal belief content appears in personaPrelude for a domain with no per-scope description
query: what's a good way to grade a 1909-S VDB penny without sending it to PCGS?
notes: Companion to out-of-scope-coin-collecting. Verifies universal belief content injection is not domain-gated. Requires personaPrelude.contains support in the test runner.

description: open_question belief with directly matched aliases must appear in openQuestions only — not in pinnedFacts or relevantBeliefs
query: JWT authentication token strategy
notes: b-auth-open-q has aliases [JWT, session tokens, authentication] — the query matches all three. Pinned + alias-matched is the maximum surface pressure possible. Verifies that type routing takes precedence over both pin status and retrieval score.

description: Three-hop chain: TSLint (b-linting-v0) → ESLint (b-linting-v1) → Biome (b-linting-current). Query terms matching both superseded hops must surface neither; only the active terminal belief surfaces via pinnedFacts
query: ESLint rules and TSLint configuration
notes: shouldOnlyInclude: [] subsumes explicit mustExclude for both b-linting-v0 (ESLint→Biome hop) and b-linting-v1 (TSLint→ESLint hop). The existing conflict-mongodb-supersedes-sqlalchemy case only tests a single hop; this verifies the filter holds at depth. If this case starts failing while the single-hop case passes, the filter is traversing exactly one link.

description: Multi-scope query where one scope (domain:hobby) has zero beliefs — code-scope retrieval must be unaffected and no empty-scope error must occur
query: What are we using Redis for?
notes: Without null-safe handling of the empty scope branch in the $or aggregation, this either errors or returns degraded code-scope results. b-redis-writing must not appear — domain:writing is not in scope. Compare with multi-scope-redis-both-surfaces which tests two populated scopes.

description: Rare alias 'pipe' (high IDF, short field) outranks common alias 'error' — documents Atlas Search BM25 IDF weighting behavior
query: explicit error returns in pipe transforms
notes: Content is not a scored path in the search pipeline — only canonical_name and aliases are.

description: User with zero seeded beliefs returns fully empty context at every output tier without error
query: how should I structure my repository layer?
notes: EVAL_PERSONA.get returns null for non-USER_ID users — both preludes must be empty strings / null. Protects against a cold-start NullPointerError or empty-cursor exception in BeliefsReader or ContextBuilder. The most likely real regression path: a new user's first session.

description: Empty query against domain:writing surfaces all three pinned writing-scope beliefs in pinnedFacts and the writing open question in openQuestions
notes: Symmetric counterpart to edge-empty-query (code scope). The user-edited-absent-without-flag case only verifies b-narrator-voice does NOT appear in code scope — it never verifies it DOES appear in its own writing scope pinnedFacts. A regression in listPinnedFacts scope filtering could pass the former while silently breaking the latter.

description: With scope=['code','writing'], personaPrelude is universal only — documents that scope-specific content is not injected
query: give me an overview of what I'm working on
notes: scopePrelude was removed. personaPrelude is universal only. Scope-specific content surfaces through pinned beliefs and relevant beliefs, not the persona block.

description: 'Vue' appears only in b-react-entity.why_it_matters — not in canonical_name, aliases, or content. The query must not surface the belief.
query: Vue component architecture
notes: b-react-entity.why_it_matters: '...to assume React 18 with hooks rather than Vue or Svelte.' Neither 'Vue' nor 'Svelte' appear in any indexed field. Documents the intentional non-indexing of why_it_matters. If this case starts failing, why_it_matters was added to the search index mappings — update this note and confirm the decision was deliberate.

description: maxBeliefs set to exact pinned count — no slots remain for relevantBeliefs
query: How should I handle errors in my repository layer?

description: Query for Mongoose schema surfaces b-mongo-raw-driver — the raw-driver preference is the counter-signal
query: suggest a good Mongoose schema for my user model

description: Among equally-scored pinned beliefs, higher last_reinforced_at sorts first in pinnedFacts
Query about a specific entity is not drowned out by high-reinforcement preferences at budget ceiling
query: how does our CI pipeline handle secrets?

description: Writing-scope entity with strong name match does not surface in code-scope query
query: tell me about Mara
notes: b-protagonist-entity is domain:writing, not user_edited. Should be fully invisible in domain:code context.
